summary: Steve Yegge reviews four console games he's played over the past year: Portal, Mario Galaxy, Zelda: Twilight Princess, and Uncharted: Drake's Fortune. He praises Portal's innovative gameplay and humor, calls Mario Galaxy possibly the best Mario since the original, admires Twilight Princess's emotional storytelling, and gushes over Uncharted's stunning visuals on his new PS3 and plasma TV. The post also includes his thoughts on console hardware preferences, particularly his enthusiasm for the Wii's innovative controls and his recent HD setup upgrade. Throughout, he maintains his characteristic conversational style while offering genuine recommendations for fellow gamers who share his taste in adventure and RPG titles.
key_points: Portal is a flawless short masterpiece with innovative gameplay and surprisingly funny writing
Mario Galaxy arguably surpasses all previous Mario titles with exceptional music, atmosphere, and level design
Twilight Princess features an emotionally powerful story arc, particularly the 'Midna's Desperate Hour' sequence
Uncharted: Drake's Fortune showcases the PS3's visual potential with graphics that surpass even Blu-Ray movies
The Wii's motion controls make traditional controllers feel like 'Atari Joysticks' from the 1980s
money_quotes: Going back to the XBox 360 or Playstation controllers feels totally rinky-dink, like I've traveled back to the 1980s and I'm using an Atari Joystick.
Mario Galaxy is arguably the best Mario title since the original Super Mario Bros. arcade game.
I've never played a game before that made it feel like all five senses were in play the way this game does.
Nothing, nothing I've seen so far on my new screen, not Blu-Ray discs, not HD channels, not Oblivion on the XBox 360, nothing can compare to the visual feast provided by Drake's Fortune.
I pretty much bought my XBox 360 for Oblivion... It didn't matter, though, since Oblivion was good enough to warrant the purchase of an XBox 360.
The game is sumptuous, no better word for it.
key_insight: Great games justify entire console purchases, and the best ones create such immersive experiences that they engage all your senses and leave lasting emotional memories.
